items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: ...I see.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Yes... I can feel the tension in the Streams.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I'm worried something bad is going to happen...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: There you are, Rover!

items.speaker: Baizhi
items.text: The Academy has noted a concerning rise in Tacet Field activities. Please use caution when exiting the city.

items.speaker: Baizhi
items.text: Remember to check your Terminal signals, and wear the Academy's protective suit correctly...

items.speaker: Baizhi
items.text: ...And don't forget to stay hydrated. Now, I have experiments to run. Take care.

options.text: Is she sending the same thing to everyone...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: It's just Baizhi being Baizhi. She's a little awkward with showing care, but I think it's cute!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Anyway! Rover, quick, tell us what happened!
After a brief moment of thinking, you gave them a brief run down of your meeting with Jinhsi, but didn’t mention Jué's disappearance...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: The Fractsidus again? Ugh, those jerks!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Jiyan the General, Jinhsi the Magistrate, and Jué, Jinzhou’s Sentinel... Is there any kind of connection between them?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Connection? ...No idea, but I did hear General Jiyan used to be a military doctor...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Hmm... Speaking of that, I think we'll need to start with a bit of Jinzhou's past.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: A storyteller near the theater once told us something like this...

dialog.stepLabel: Chixia: "Woah. First time hearing about all that......"

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Woah. First time hearing about all that...

options.text: Why did Jué choose Jinhsi to be the Magistrate?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Uhhh... Maybe the Sentinel had its own special ways of picking candidates?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Or maybe it is their incomprehensible ways that set these divine beings apart from us? Their thinking tends to be elusive to the average person.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Yeah, true. Wouldn't be surprised if Jué decided to make me the Magistrate one day, not gonna lie!

options.text: Does it ever ask the opinion of the person selected?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I understand your concern.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Madam Jinhsi was only a teenager, and she was suddenly selected to be the Magistrate. It must have been so difficult for her...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I remember people were skeptical when she first took office.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Despite her achievements, many believed she lacked the necessary experience and authority in the political realm.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: People only showed their respect to Madam Jinhsi because they trusted Jué's judgment...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Seriously, Lord Jué knows how to choose 'em. Our previous Magistrates were all top-notch at running the show.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: And Madam Jinhsi? She's already made this city a well-oiled machine in no time.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: I'd have a mental Overclock just trying to keep up with all that paperwork... She was made for this!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Uh, what is that saying again? Real gold never loses its shine!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Rover, since you are asking about this... Did you get clues about your past from Madam Jinhsi?

options.text: Jué asked me to go find Jiyan in the Norfall Barrens at a specific time.
There's another "Overseer" of the Fractsidus lurking in Jinzhou.
An organization called the Black Shores has been watching me.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: The Norfall Barrens?! It's been a battlefield since ancient times...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Rover, it's dangerous there. The Norfall Barrens is the frontline in our defense against the Threnodian. We must be careful about going there.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: They sent two Overseers at once... Their scheme must be far more expansive than we thought.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Though the Fractsidus do seem to know a lot about you... Their ideology and actions are too dangerous.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: And right now, the odds are in their favor. If we rush into a confrontation, it could lead us into their traps.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: The Black Shores? Doesn't ring a bell... Sounds like the name of a place, but I've never seen it on any map.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: This flower... It's only a photo, and I can't tell where it came from with my ability.

options.text: The Black Shores is an organization that studies the Lament.
Every Black Shores member wears a flower like this.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Hmm... We're still in the dark about Fractsidus' plans, and the Norfall Barrens is too risky. The Black Shores seems to be our only option now.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: But if we are to look for them with only a photo of this flower... It's going to be difficult.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Since it's about flowers... We should go ask Grandma Lin!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Yes! Grandma Lin runs a flower shop. I'm sure she can give us some tips.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Hehe... You're making me blush a little.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Grandma Lin loves listening to storytellers. I think I saw her earlier... Let's go look for her.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Hello... Are you lost? Do you need help?

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Oops, sorry! I should've introduced myself first... I know it's a polite thing to do in Huanglong...

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: ...Starflower? That's what Grandma Lin likes to call me...

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Oops, sorry! I should've introduced myself first... I know it's a polite thing to do in Huanglong...

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: It is… It is a delight to make your acquaintance. I am Verina. My "pressure" to have the opportunity to meet with you today…

options.text: What if we were some bad guys? Aren't you worried?

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: My apologies. I used to live in the New Federation, so I'm not very familiar with the language here.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: My apologies. I used to live in the New Federation, so I'm not very familiar with the etiquette here.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Eh? Really? But that flower next to you said she likes you a lot...

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Plants don't lie. I trust their words, so you can't be bad guys.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Hi, Verina! I'm Chixia, this is Yangyang, and {Male=he;Female=she}'s Rover! Grandma Lin told us we should come find you!

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Oh, right. Grandma Lin talked about you. I left those flowers so she knows how to reach me.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: What seems to be the trouble? If there's anything I can help you with...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: We're looking for a special flower...

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Hmm... It's an unusual flower indeed. Pure black flowers are rarely found in nature. We can't rule out the possibility that it's been dyed.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: When looking at the structure, there are a few types of flowers that seem similar.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Take Iris, for example. Its petals are similarly oval-shaped, but it has double petals.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: It's not Viola, either, since Viola has notched petals.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Then there's also the Cliffrecluse, but this flower has a longer stamen and an extra petal.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Lemongrass is also similar in shape. It's native to the New Federation, and Huanglong people may not be familiar with it.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Though it has a similar stamen length, it has a conical inflorescence with a trumpet-shaped corolla.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Oh, and I almost forgot the Pecok Flower! It's a single-petaled flower with a similar stamen length!

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: But... The Pecok Flower has a distinct peduncle, while this one doesn't.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Wow, you really know your stuff! I learned a lot from that!

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Anyway, this black flower does look very special. There are hardly any similar plants in nature.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: So, I'm thinking it might be a newly cultivated species.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Also, its veins are somehow cross-shaped... I can feel some faint traces of frequency fluctuations on it too. It's a sign of overflowing Remnant Energy.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: So, my guess is it should be an unique Remnant plant, created by a certain type of Resonance Energy.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: But in order to figure out the right soil for it, or exactly what type of Resonance Energy it is, I need more than just a picture.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Gosh, help us out, someone! We don't know what kind of flower it is, and we haven't seen anyone wearing it either...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Speaking of that... How does it even work? If I wore this flower on me right now, do I automatically become one of them?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Chixia, wait, what did you just say?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Uhhh... Yeah? I was just saying if I wore this flower, would they immediately see me as one of their own...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: If this black flower is indeed their symbol, the Black Shores members should immediately notice it on you.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: But to all the other people, it would only look like a peculiar flower.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Given that... if they are indeed in Jinzhou city, we may only need to wear this flower to lure them out...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Ohh, I see... but how do we get our hands on one?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: ...Ha, now you're here.

options.text: Where are you going to run off to this time?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Well, we weren't really running from you! We just wanted to make sure you were alone.

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Be good, please!

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Encore and Uncle Aalto won't do anything bad to you...

options.text: What do you want from me?!
She just called you an uncle again!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: That's enough Encore, you're freaking our precious client out.

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Encore just did as Aalto said! Encore is usually a good girl.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Don't be scared, Rover. We just have a few questions for you.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Was it your idea to have those two ladies wear those counterfeit Blake Blooms and attract our attention?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: I must admit, they looked quite convincing. If I was an outsider, I might mistake them for the real deal.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: So you worked with a plant-type Resonator... Interesting.

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: A plant-type Resonator? We happen to know someone like that too, don't we?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Yeah. There are more than one of them out there. Not a big deal.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: So please, tell us... What do you want from the Black Shores?

options.text: My past might be related to the Black Shores.
Now you're finally biting the hook.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Why? Hmm, what if I told you... We have the information you want the most?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Oh? Really? So that lead about you was right indeed...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Haha, not bad, you've managed to catch on real quick.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Like what they say here in Huanglong: the straight hook is for willing fish to bite. Ta-daa da daah! Your efforts finally caught our attention.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Surprise! The people you've been looking for are right in front of you!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Now, how's that for a piece of intel? I'll let you have it free of charge!

options.text: You're both from the Black Shores?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Aww, not that disappointed look! Are we not what you had in mind?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Aww, not that disappointed look! Gotta learn how to enjoy a surprise while you can, or life gets real boring, you know?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: I know you must have a ton of questions for me, but one moment, please.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: As you already know, I'm an "information broker".

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: That means I make deals instead of passing out freebies. You want information from me, you gotta give me something in return.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Relax, Rover. We just want to make a deal with you.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: We want you to come with us to an abandoned Court of Savantae facility, to help us recover a "Resonance Beacon".

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: In turn, I'll tell you everything we know so far.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Our business is centered around customer satisfaction and built on integrity! Otherwise, we wouldn't qualify as Consultants of the Black Shores.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Well, if you don't take this opportunity, you'll probably never get to learn more about the Black Shores again.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: And since you're a first-time customer... I'm pleased to offer you an additional gift.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Here, have this. This should prove we're serious, since you already know what it represents.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: And if you still have doubts about us... Just show it to that plant-savvy young miss. She'll be able to tell.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Finally, as a last resort...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Encore, do your cute face, quick!

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Aww... Pwease help us... Pretty Pweeaaase...?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Look at this poor child, pleading to you like that! How could anyone have the heart to decline?! The deal's settled then!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: We'll be waiting for you near this Beacon in Whining Aix's Mire! And don't forget to come alone, okay?

dialog.stepLabel: ??: "You're such a box full of surprises...."

items.text: You're such a box full of surprises.
So we're playing truth-or-dare now? I see... Oops!
Hehe... Getting angry, are we? Sorry, but it's just so much fun to rile you up.
You're the very first person to pique my interest like this. You... have great potential.
Okay, so you're not up for small talk. I'll keep that in mind. You're my mission target, after all.

options.text: Are you from the Black Shores?

items.text: Bingo! I'm one of the Bloom Bearers of the Black Shores.
This Blake Bloom is our symbol. It represents our mission to save the world.
That's what it says in the rules, at least.

options.text: What are you planning to do with me?

items.text: Our plans? Hmm... What is it again? To recruit as many talents as we can, and save the world from its impending doom.
So, are you in?

options.text: You don't sound like you want to save the world.

items.text: Nuh-uh, I don't! The world? Couldn't care less. If it's all going BOOM tomorrow, why not have fun while we can?
To stop at nothing for more power, take down any prey without mercy, and indulge in all our desires for the ultimate pleasure...

options.text: How are you any different from the Fractsidus then...

items.text: Well... I mean, I'd love to live that way, but alas, duty calls.
<ano=The Black Shores>Our "Garden"</ano> is home to many green fruits, and we'll make you the most special "Seed" among them all...
My duty is to nurture and safeguard seeds like you, allowing them to blossom into luscious fruits.
Then once the fruit ripens, I'll get to pick it, and indulge the sweetness of my labor all by myself.

You're just like what <ano=Civilization Simulation Sand Table>Tethys</ano> has told us... I can feel such explosive potential within you. One day, you'll surely bear the most beautiful fruit imaginable.
Hehe... I like how cold you sound. You're tempting me to push your buttons a bit more...
Relax, relax. I'll behave for the time being.
No need to be so on edge. I won't do anything... strange, to you, I think?
Actually, I've been diligently protecting you, you know? I got rid of all types of pests that tried to get in your way!

Ew, don't mention me and the Fractsidus in the same sentence. I couldn't care less about their petty games, but if they even think of laying their dirty fingers on you... Hmph. You're mine, and I don't like sharing.
Just a couple tricks and I had those fools flapping around like headless chickens. What a boring crowd.
Woah, don't tell me you haven't noticed?
Enjoyed your nightly rendezvous with that Loong Maiden? You have me to thank for keeping those Fractsidus people away.
Well... I've answered plenty of questions already. Now it's your turn.

options.text: What do you want to ask?
Is this how you treat the talents you're trying to recruit?

items.text: Hehe, nothing in particular.
The way you look at me though, it's almost as if... No, forget about it.

options.text: We've been in this position for long enough now.

items.text: ...Cough... Do you think I'd entertain... your inquiries, if I didn't have a blade on my throat?...
Anyway, I'm not trying to get any answers from you...
But I'll be watching you. You're a very special "Seed", that I am very sure.
I can feel something oddly familiar about you...

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: My name is Camellya.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Try not to let me catch you so easily next time~!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: And she's gone... Huh, that was fast.

dialog.stepLabel: Yangyang: "Rover, what took you so long to pick up the call? Did someth..."

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Rover, what took you so long to pick up the call? Did something happen?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Is that a friend or foe? Are you hurt?

options.text: I am safe, thank you for asking.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: The Black Shores... Did she tell you anything?

options.text: A shame. I let her get away.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Phew... At least you are safe.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Phew... At least you are safe.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Chixia noticed a guy acting suspicious. I think she's keeping an eye on him.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Sure, we are in the shopping district. See you then.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Rover, did you find them?

options.text: Yes. They're from the Black Shores.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: What? Seriously? No way...

options.text: They gave me this Blake Bloom.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: As I expected! This flower is indeed a type of Remnant plant.

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: And it contains a very unique kind of Remnant Energy. The concentration is much higher than usual, too.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: No way, are they really from the Black Shores?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Tsk... Not as mysterious as people make 'em out to be. Well at least they're good at running, I'll give them that.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: What did they tell you?

options.text: I made a deal with them.

items.text: You explained the current situation...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: That place... I only heard it's an abandoned research institution. There's nothing special about it.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Because of the dangerous weather conditions in Whining Aix's Mire, it's been ages since anyone went there.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Don't worry, Yangyang. We all know how strong our Rover is!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Okay... Rover, I'll tell the Academy about this, and they'll help transmit your data from the Resonance Nexus. Then that Beacon in the Whining Aix's Mire should be unlocked for you.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: There have been frequent Tacet Field activities recently. If you're going there alone, please be careful.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Just ring us whenever you need a hand! We'll come rushing!

dialog.stepLabel: Chixia: "Rover, perfect timing! This guy's been sniffin' around here ..."

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Rover, perfect timing! This guy's been sniffin' around here for a while now. He keeps trying to talk to the elderly and minors! I've been keeping an eye on him!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: And I saw a little girl with him! She kept turning her head and looking around, but now she's nowhere to be found. This is super fishy!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Hey, I'm Patroller Chixia. Sir, business license, please.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: B-Business license? Uuuhh... Oh! I'm Aalto, just a humble information broker. I've been travelling around, helping people out in my own small way...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: I see. So, no license. I bet you don't have a fixed business location either, huh?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: ...Business location? Why would I need that?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Hmph, now show me your Organizational Accreditation, please.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Well... I am an honest broker of information, always open to negotiations at the right price! I'm the all-time Intel champ, with more Best Seller certificates than I can count...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: No license, no clear business plan... You're running an illegal business, then...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Who's your supervisor? You should at least have their ID number?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: My s-supervisor? Oh right! She's, like, a super big deal!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: She's an ancient being, born with this planet we're living on. She's the will of the star, the reverberation of everything in this world...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: So do you even have a supervisor or not?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: What? C'mon, I already told you about her! Why won't you believe me?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Yeah, yeah! I believe you! I know what's going on!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Now, sir, you're coming with me to the Patroller Station!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Oh, come on! Please, I didn't do anything illegal! I was just looking for someone!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: A little girl, um, about this tall... She's the kind to stand out in any crowd!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: You really think I'm gonna buy that?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Ugh, you know! You saw her earlier! It's that girl!

options.text: She seems to bounce while she walks?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Yeah! Exactly! Wait, how do you know?

dialog.stepLabel: Verina: "Oh, please watch out... It's dangerous to run like that......"

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Oh, please watch out... It's dangerous to run like that...

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Here you are! Hurray!

dialog.stepLabel: Aalto: "Encore! I looked everywhere for you!..."

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Encore! I looked everywhere for you!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Back off! Leave that girl alone!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Miss Patroller, she's the girl I've been looking for!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Hello, little one. Is your name Encore? Do you know this person?

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Yes! Encore knows him!

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Uncle Aalto sells stuff without a license, he listens in on people for commercial secrets, and he loves pulling pranks on us kids... But he’s not a bad guy, really!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Ugh, Encore! Remember our little chat?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: What did I say about calling me "Uncle" in public again? I am NOT that old!

options.text: That doesn't add up with what you just said.
You sound really suspicious to me.
Is *that* what you're gonna focus on?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Miss Verina, where did you meet this girl?

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: I ran into her near a cliff on my way home. She was eating a poisonous mushroom...

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: I managed to make her the antidote in time. Imagine what would have happened if I wasn't there...

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Don't worry! Encore's had plenty of that mushroom already!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: But why did you go to such a dangerous place alone, little Encore?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: We've got your back, little one! No worries! Just tell us what you know!

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Because... Because Encore must keep an eye on {Male=him;Female=her}!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Ahahaha... Kids can have such wild imaginations sometimes, haha...

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Hey, Aalto? Didn't you send Encore there? Wait... Why are they all giving you the side eye?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Encore... Some things are better left unspoken, you know...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Since the situation has come to this, I've got no choice but to tell you...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Indeed! We're here for business. And it's a crucial one! You wanna know what it is?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Let's go, Encore!

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Hey, Aalto! Wait for meeee!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: See ya! We still have a world to save!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Cough cough... Wait!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: This is the place.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Ugh, those savants really love their puzzles, don't they...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: No wonder folks never managed to get inside after all these years. Their security is top-notch.

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: That's because Aalto set off the security alarm! And now the gate's closed, hmph!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Ahem... Anyway! We've got to figure out how to open it up again.

dialog.stepLabel: Encore: "But why did they keep that big guy here?..."

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: But why did they keep that big guy here?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: According to our findings earlier, researchers who worked here were probably only a small faction of the Court of Savantae Committee.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: They were likely studying an ancient civilization they deemed to be "divine beings", but later got denounced from the CSC due to their extreme methods.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Somehow, they believed this ancient civilization had once visited the Mire, which led to their attempts at recreating those past events with artificial Retroact Rain.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: And when they failed to recreate the Retroact Rain, they resorted to making a "replica" of the lost civilization instead.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: To get their desired results faster, they started feeding this "replica" various frequencies, datasets, and mechanical constructs...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: And we've seen what that ended up with. It turned into a monster driven by the frequencies of "hunger," devouring everything in its path.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: It was probably the culprit behind the disappearance of those folks.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: And, I suspect, the Fractsidus Overseer mentioned in that log may have something to do with it all.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Those researchers... They were too obsessed with validating the "divine miracle", they eventually got lured in by that monster's frequencies of hunger and willingly walked into its maw...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: The monster devoured them, but not with its teeth... Their own delusions and fear were their undoing in the end.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: What a crazy bunch...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: So Rover, now it's time for me to fulfill my promise.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: What do you want to know about the Black Shores?

options.text: What on earth does the Black Shores do?
Do you know a person named Camellya?
What are you doing in Jinzhou?
What is the relationship between me and the Black Shores?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: As you already know, we monitor and research the Lament, and we’ve got Resonance Beacons set up in every nation worldwide.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: The island our headquarters are built on is in fact a massive piece of Tacetite ore, which powers our various energy-intensive operations.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: This Blake Bloom is a product of that overflowing energy on the island.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Our island is located in the perfect spot to accurately monitor the Lament, ensuring precise predictions.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: ...That being said, the Lament's behavior is constantly changing and we are still studying to understand its true nature.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: All we can do for now is to observe the Lament, study its patterns, and provide early warnings to countries worldwide.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Alright, alright, no more beating around the bush. Yeah, we've had our eyes on you, but please trust me, we don't mean anything bad.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: We just wanted to make sure you're a dependable ally.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: The Black Shores members all work together to save the world from the Lament crisis. We've got talents from all walks of life, including skilled hackers, explosive experts, and even Fengshui masters... You name it, we have it.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: We like your skills and methods. So, what do you say? Ready to jump on board?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Camellya? Course we do!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Yeah, she can be kinda unhinged, but she's indeed one of ours.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Just so you know, she only took up this mission because of you! Quite the charmer, aren't you?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: So you've met her already? Nice, saves me some time introducing.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: I know she can be a bit too... straightforward, in how she expresses herself, but... Don't worry. She won't do anything bad to you, really.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: We just received a warning from our Lament detection system. Another Threnodian is about to revive near Jinzhou city.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: So we came here to inform Jinzhou's Magistrate about it.

options.text: Did similar things happen in the past?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Yes. Entire countries had been destroyed... We're not letting such tragedies happen again.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Yes. Entire countries had been destroyed... We're not letting such tragedies happen again.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: I'm just an insignificant Consultant at the Black Shores, so I don't know that much about all its hidden secrets.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: But I believe the piece of intel I'm about to share with you will make this trip worth your effort.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: This is not the first time you've been reawakened. Last time... You woke up at the Black Shores.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Rover, we share the same goal.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: We're both aiming to fight against the Threnodians and prevent the Lament crisis.

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Yep! Encore can prove it! The Threnodian is a bad, bad guy, and Encore and Aalto are the heroes of justice, so we're gonna stop the bad guys!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: I understand. Trust isn't something you can afford to give out that easily.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Remember the Blake Bloom I gave you earlier?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: It is more than just a symbol. It also holds the key to our headquarters. I'm sure it will come in handy for you.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Phew! Anyway, mission accomplished! We've got the Resonance Beacon secured, plus a sweet bonus of Court of Savantae's big secrets!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Glad I got you on board, Rover. Looking forward to our future collaborations.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Oops, almost forgot! Gotta check that Resonance Beacon again, just in case.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Wait... This is...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: No, it can't be...

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Hey, Aalto! WAAITT!

dialog.stepLabel: Verina: "Erm, I... I could probably make one for you with my Resonanc..."

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Erm, I... I could probably make one for you with my Resonance Ability... If you just need something that looks similar...

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: Come on, Verina, you can do it...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: No way? That's amazing, seriously! It looks exactly like the flower in that picture!

items.speaker: Verina
items.text: T-thank you, it's nothing... I'm glad I could help.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: It's friggin amazing! Could you do that again? Please! I love it!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Better hide here for now.)

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hmm... Where is {Male=he;Female=she} hiding?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Looking for me?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Ooh, here you are.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: This is... No wonder...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Those crazy savants... They were trying to recreate the Retroact Rain...

dialog.stepLabel: Aalto: "Never thought they could pull off something like this... Inc..."

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Never thought they could pull off something like this... Incredible...

options.text: What on earth is the Retroact Rain?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: The Retroact Rain is essentially a form of condensed Remnant Energy from the Etheric Sea.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Various events across space and time from every dimension are recorded in the form of Remnant Energy. The Retroact Rain conjures illusions of past events for those exposed to it.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: That rain curtain we saw earlier was probably the results of their experiments.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Before the Retroact Rain forms, it goes through three stages. In the first stage, it looks just like any regular rain.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: And what we're seeing now should be the second stage: Water droplets floating in midair, being absolutely still.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: I've heard of plenty independant studies on the Retroact Rain.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Most of the researchers were driven by their obsessions. Some wanted to relive a particular experience, while others yearned to reunite with their loved ones...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: But look at all these devices here... The sheer scale of this research is really something.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: It really makes one wonder... What on earth were they trying to bring back with such a massive Retroact Rain?

dialog.stepLabel: Elder Male Voice: "...It's getting closer. These will be my final words, haha....."

items.speaker: Elder Male Voice
items.text: ...It's getting closer. These will be my final words, haha... If only I had more time...

items.speaker: Elder Male Voice
items.text: I thought my enemies were misconceptions, waning passion, and my own ignorance.

items.speaker: Elder Male Voice
items.text: Only in the end did I realize... time is my ultimate foe.

items.speaker: Elder Male Voice
items.text: I became blindsided by my fear of death and insatiable thirst for truth, eventually derailing our research...

items.speaker: Elder Male Voice
items.text: It was too late when I finally came back to my senses... We... We had already walked into that false "Gate"...

items.speaker: Elder Male Voice
items.text: Now, I'm leaving my last realization behind...

items.speaker: Elder Male Voice
items.text: They are our "past"... but they are also our "future"... Had I realized it sooner, there might have been a chance to get things back on track...

items.speaker: Elder Male Voice
items.text: The future being hailing from our past... Please save us... once again...

items.speaker: Elder Male Voice
items.text: Please, keep pushing forward... until you reach... the real Gate that can change the world...

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Wow... She's so pretty...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Rover, about this person...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Heh, knew it...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Great, now that power's been restored, that elevator should work.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: The Beacon's signal is coming from below. Let's go.

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Wow! A big cluncky monster!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Damn... Those savants never run out of new surprises for us...

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: The signal's coming from it. Did it swallow our Resonance Beacon?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Looks like peace isn't an option. Guys, brace yourself. We'll need to take it down!

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Aalto, we doing it like we usually do?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Of course.

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Hehe, then this big guy is Encore's to tackle!

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Cosmos, Cloudy, ATTACK!

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Phew, job done!

items.speaker: Encore
items.text: Hehe, Encore is strong! Told ya!
